title: Developer-to-production SoD — same developer submits AND approves merge
exclusions: Does NOT cover intra-sprint experimental branches not deployed to production.
evidence: Saved search 'dev_prod_sod' (hourly) emits merges whose author == approver for production branches.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Direct evidence of SoD deficiency in the deployment pipeline.
CC6.3 logical access authorisation — SoD in code path.
A.8.30 outsourced development and secure development lifecycle.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A merge to main on repo core-app has author==approver; the UC fires within the hour.

dataSources: Git provider audit logs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket), CI/CD deployment events.
spl:
index=git sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=gitlab:audit OR sourcetype=bitbucket:audit action=merge earliest=-1h
| search target_branch IN (main,master,release*)
| where author=approver
| table _time repo target_branch author approver commit_sha
description: Direct SoD control for merge-to-production: author and approver must be distinct users.
value: Makes a core deployment-pipeline SoD requirement a continuously-enforced technical control.
implementation: (1) Ensure Git provider captures author + approver; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, revert merge via pipeline automation.
visualization: Table of violations, bar chart by repo, single-value 'merges SoD-compliant (30d)'.
knownFalsePositives: Solo-maintainer repos legitimately have author=approver; these must carry a risk-acceptance annotation.
